Package com.wip.security

Class CustomUserDetails

java.lang.Object
com.wip.security.CustomUserDetails
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
public class CustomUserDetails extends Object implements org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
Custom implementation of Spring Security's UserDetails for the Courier Tracking System.

This class serves as the bridge between the application's AppUser domain entity and the Spring Security authentication framework. It wraps an AppUser instance and exposes the fields required by Spring Security (username, password, and granted authorities) alongside application-specific accessors for the user's internal ID, associated customer ID, and role string.

The granted authority is derived from the AppUser.getRole() value by prepending the ROLE_ prefix required by Spring Security's role-based access control (e.g., a role of "ADMIN" becomes the authority "ROLE_ADMIN").

All account-status flags (accountNonExpired, accountNonLocked, credentialsNonExpired, and enabled) unconditionally return true, meaning all accounts are considered active and valid at all times.

Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Dharshan K S (dharshan.ks@wipro.com)
See Also:

    Returns the underlying AppUser domain entity wrapped by this instance.

    Returns the unique identifier of the customer profile associated with this user, if any.

    Returns the BCrypt-encoded password of the authenticated user as stored in the database.

    Returns the unique internal identifier of the authenticated user.

    Indicates whether the user's account has expired.

    Indicates whether the user's account is locked.

    Indicates whether the user's credentials (password) have expired.

    Indicates whether the user is enabled or disabled.

  • Constructor Details

    • CustomUserDetails

      public CustomUserDetails(AppUser user)
      Constructs a new CustomUserDetails wrapping the given AppUser.
      Parameters:
      user - the application user entity to wrap; must not be null

  • Method Details

    • getUserId

      public Long getUserId()
      Returns the unique internal identifier of the authenticated user.

      This is the primary key (userId) of the AppUser entity and can be used to perform user-specific data queries within the application.

      Returns:
      the Long primary key of the wrapped AppUser

    • getCustomerId

      public Long getCustomerId()
      Returns the unique identifier of the customer profile associated with this user, if any.

      A user may or may not have a linked customer profile. If the AppUser has an associated Customer entity, its customerId is returned; otherwise null is returned.

      Returns:
      the Long customer ID if a customer profile is linked to this user, or null if no customer profile exists

    • getRole

      public String getRole()
      Returns the raw role string assigned to this user as stored in the data source.

      This value is the plain role name without the ROLE_ prefix (e.g., "ADMIN" or "USER"). For the Spring Security authority representation with the prefix, refer to getAuthorities().

      Returns:
      the role string of the wrapped AppUser (e.g., "ADMIN" or "USER")

    • getAppUser

      public AppUser getAppUser()
      Returns the underlying AppUser domain entity wrapped by this instance.

      Use this accessor when full access to the application user's domain-level properties is required beyond what is exposed by the UserDetails interface.

      Returns:
      the AppUser entity wrapped by this CustomUserDetails

    • getAuthorities

      public Collection<? extends org.springframework.security.core.GrantedAuthority> getAuthorities()
      Returns the collection of granted authorities assigned to the authenticated user.

      The authority is derived from the user's role by prefixing it with ROLE_, as required by Spring Security's role-based access control conventions. For example, a user with role "ADMIN" is granted the authority "ROLE_ADMIN".

      Specified by:
      getAuthorities in interface org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
      Returns:
      a singleton List containing a SimpleGrantedAuthority constructed from the user's role prefixed with "ROLE_"

    • getPassword

      public String getPassword()
      Returns the BCrypt-encoded password of the authenticated user as stored in the database.

      This value is used by Spring Security's DaoAuthenticationProvider to verify the raw password supplied at login against the stored hash.

      Specified by:
      getPassword in interface org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
      Returns:
      the BCrypt-encoded password hash of the wrapped AppUser

    • getUsername

      public String getUsername()
      Returns the username used to authenticate the user.

      This value is used as the principal identifier throughout Spring Security's authentication and authorization pipeline.

      Specified by:
      getUsername in interface org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
      Returns:
      the username of the wrapped AppUser

    • isAccountNonExpired

      public boolean isAccountNonExpired()
      Indicates whether the user's account has expired.

      This implementation always returns true, indicating that accounts never expire in this application.

      Specified by:
      isAccountNonExpired in interface org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
      Returns:
      true unconditionally

    • isAccountNonLocked

      public boolean isAccountNonLocked()
      Indicates whether the user's account is locked.

      This implementation always returns true, indicating that accounts are never locked in this application.

      Specified by:
      isAccountNonLocked in interface org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
      Returns:
      true unconditionally

    • isCredentialsNonExpired

      public boolean isCredentialsNonExpired()
      Indicates whether the user's credentials (password) have expired.

      This implementation always returns true, indicating that credentials never expire in this application.

      Specified by:
      isCredentialsNonExpired in interface org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
      Returns:
      true unconditionally

    • isEnabled

      public boolean isEnabled()
      Indicates whether the user is enabled or disabled.

      This implementation always returns true, indicating that all registered users are enabled and can authenticate without any additional activation step.

      Specified by:
      isEnabled in interface org.springframework.security.core.userdetails.UserDetails
      Returns:
      true unconditionally
